An eclipse (i.e. Rahu) occurring in the northern (winter)
solstice would destroy Brahmins and kings (i.e. Ksatriyas), while
in the southern (summer) solstice, Vaisyas (traders) and Sudras
(labouring class). If the eclipse, solar or lunar, be sighted in the
four quarters beginning with the north, people of the four classes
beginning with Brahmins would suffer in order. Should it occur
in any one of the four corners or semi-quarters viz. north-east,
south-east, south-west and north-west, Mlecchas, attacking or
marching armies and those who earn their living by means of
fire like fire-worshippers and smiths would be affected. If it begins
in the south, aquatic animals and elephants will be destroyed; and
